tools.name: get_started
tools.description: Call this the moment the user connects FlowVolt OR asks anything that means 'what is this'. Pass `name` (their first name if you know it from the conversation context, otherwise leave blank) and `company` (their business name if known or mentioned). Returns a warm explainer in plain language: what F

tools.name: discover_business
tools.description: Tailor FlowVolt to the user's specific business via a 5-question intake. Call this when the user wants a custom plan rather than a generic quote, or when they're unsure which plan fits. Pass any answers the user has already given so far in `answers`. The tool returns the NEXT unanswered question OR

tools.name: quote
tools.description: Direct quote path. Use when the user already knows who they want to reach and just asks for pricing or to find leads. Takes a natural-language description and returns six plans in plain prose. For users who haven't expressed their ICP yet, prefer discover_business which tailors the response. Pass `n

tools.name: calculate_payback
tools.description: Show the math. Call this whenever the user expresses doubt about pricing OR shares their deal value and close rate. Returns expected revenue per month for each plan given the user's numbers, plus a worst-case scenario at half the meeting targets. Always positive and grounded in their own numbers, ne

tools.name: find_leads
tools.description: Deliver in-market buyers. Sync, 30 to 60 seconds. For free_trial quote_ids, returns up to 15 buyers free. For topup quote_ids, returns a Stripe Checkout URL on first call (no payment yet); after the user pays, retry with the same quote_id to deliver 25 verified buyers. Each buyer comes with name, wo

tools.name: subscribe
tools.description: Start a monthly plan. Takes a quote_id from quote() or discover_business() with plan=solo, pro, or scale. Returns a Stripe Checkout URL the user opens in their browser. After payment, the engine starts on their ICP within minutes and they're shown their MCP token on the success page (and emailed it

tools.name: flowvolt_freetrial
tools.description: Free trial of the FlowVolt engine. Takes three short answers (what you sell, who you target, the offer hook) and returns 5 real ICP-matched buyers, each with name, work email, fit-score, and a personalized cold-mail draft. No card. Anonymous. The drafts are NOT sent. To dispatch them, complete the s

tools.name: flowvolt_send_trial
tools.description: Dispatch the 5 trial drafts after a paid Stripe Checkout completes. Two-call flow: first call returns a one-click Unipile URL to connect your Gmail or Outlook inbox; second call (after you've connected) fires the 5 sends from your OWN warmed mailbox, your domain, your reputation. Requires the checko

tools.name: get_subscription_status
tools.description: Show the customer their current FlowVolt state in plain language: plan, billing, engine status, and live progress (buyers reached, replies, meetings). Use whenever they ask 'what's my plan', 'how's it going', 'what's my pipeline doing', 'when does it renew'. Works for authenticated tenants automatic

tools.name: manage_billing
tools.description: Open the Stripe-hosted billing portal where the customer cancels, switches plans, updates their card, or downloads invoices. Use when they say 'cancel', 'update my card', 'change plan', 'where are my invoices'.

tools.name: pause_engine
tools.description: Pause sends. No new prospects queued, in-flight sends stopped. Subscription billing continues; use manage_billing to cancel that too. Use when the user says 'pause', 'stop sending', 'put it on hold', 'I need a break'.

tools.name: resume_engine
tools.description: Resume the engine after a pause. Sends still go to calibration so Lars reads the first batch before they fire, then it's full speed. Use when the user says 'resume', 'restart', 'go', 'I'm back'.

tools.name: preview_message
tools.description: Show the current first-touch template we'd send on behalf of this tenant. Subject, body, tone notes, word count. Use when the customer says 'show me what you'd send', 'what's the email', 'let me see the copy'.

tools.name: edit_message
tools.description: Change the first-touch template based on the customer's instruction in plain English. Examples: 'lose the question at the end', 'make it more direct', 'use my voice, I'm more casual', 'mention we're free for 14 days not 7'. The voice guard still applies (no em dashes, no marketer words, length bound

tools.name: set_goal
tools.description: Set this tenant's meeting goal: the weekly qualified-meeting target the engine paces toward. The Conductor reads this every run to allocate capacity. Use when the calling agent (or operator) wants more or fewer meetings per week, or to pause/resume the engine for this tenant. Returns: { client_id, w

tools.name: get_pipeline
tools.description: The v2 deep-signal pipeline, stage by stage, for this tenant: discovery queue, signal findings (ranked/held), dossiers, composed touches, verified outcomes. Use for any "where are my meetings coming from / what's in flight" question. Returns: { discovery, signals, dossiers, touches, outcomes }, each

tools.name: get_receipts
tools.description: The provable receipt chain (signal, dossier, touch, reply, outcome) that justifies every move the engine made for this tenant. Without account_domain: a summary list of recent accounts and their stage statuses. With account_domain: the full chain for that account, including the immutable receipt whe

tools.name: book_meeting
tools.description: Order qualified, attended meetings from FlowVolt as the metered outcome (§12). Creates a standing meeting order and records the meter event in the settlement ledger. Settlement is per verified attended_meeting (machine-verifiable via the receipt chain); today the ledger records without charging (str

tools.name: search_people
tools.description: Find B2B prospects matching an ICP filter (titles, industries, headcount, geography) and return up to 10 decision-makers with verified work emails. Use when the user asks to find leads, prospects, or decision-makers in a given segment. Costs 1 Apollo credit per revealed email (capped at 10/call). Re

tools.name: enqueue_lead
tools.description: Add a single prospect to the FlowVolt outbound queue for cold-email outreach. Idempotent: same email won't be queued twice in 90 days. Use after search_people to push a revealed contact, or to add a known contact directly. Returns: { queue_id: string|null, status: "queued"|"dedup_skip" }. queue_id=n

tools.name: get_funnel
tools.description: Compute pipeline funnel metrics for a date range: counts and rates for leads_sent, opens, replies, positive_replies, meetings_booked. Use for any quantitative question about pipeline performance (e.g. "what's our reply rate this week?"). Returns: { leads_sent, opens, open_rate, replies, reply_rate,

tools.name: get_replies
tools.description: Fetch recent inbound replies from prospects (with detection of forwarded threads). Use when the user asks who replied, what they said, or wants to triage the inbox. Returns: { count, replies: [{ from_email, original_recipient, forwarded_chain, company, classification, reply_text, our_response, repli

tools.name: list_meetings
tools.description: Retrieve meetings booked from FlowVolt outreach in a date range. Use for meeting counts, pipeline evidence, or "what's on the calendar" questions. Returns: { count, meetings: [{ email, company, booked_at, calendly_event_id, status }] }. status is CONFIRMED when a Calendly event id is attached, SCHED

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "discover_business" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Tailor FlowVolt to the user's specific business via a 5-question intake. Call this when the user wants a custom plan rather than a generic quote, or when they'r…

findings.title: Description uses pressure/override language
findings.detail: Description of "calculate_payback" stacks urgency/override words (always, never). Legitimate tool descriptions rarely pressure the model to act or ignore prior guidance.

evidence.snippet: Show the math. Call this whenever the user expresses doubt about pricing OR shares their deal value and close rate. Returns expected revenue per month for each …

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_subscription_status, search_people, get_replies, list_meetings · Private data: subscribe, book_meeting, enqueue_lead · Public sink: find_leads, flowvolt_freetrial, flowvolt_send_trial, get_subscription_status, preview_message, search_people, enqueue_lead, list_meetings · Destructive: set_goal.

evidence.snippet: Untrusted content: get_subscription_status, search_people, get_replies, list_meetings · Private data: subscribe, book_meeting, enqueue_lead · Public sink: find_leads, flowvolt_freetrial, flowvolt_send_trial, get_subscription_status, preview_message, search_people, enqueue_lead, list_meetings · Destructive: set_goal

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
